Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

maha mantra

Sadhana · asked by user [] · 2007-09-05 · 10 answers
what is the correct pronunciation of the Hare Krishna maha mantra, is the name Rama spoken as Ram or as 2 syllable RA-MA.
user [33] · 2007-09-05
2 syllable
user [166] · 2007-09-06
I know in the recordings of Srila Prabhupadas Japa, he pronounces the name Rama as "Ram"...is there a sankrit pronounciation consideration here?
user [2] · 2007-09-06
I heard Jayadvaita Swami say that both are correct in sanskrit: Ram and Rama, but I would prefer to chant the full name, just in case.
you know, manda sumanda matayo...
http://www.library108.com/media-library/HH-Jayadvaita-Swami/Seminars/Kirtan_Reform_Seminar_mar-2004/
user [154] · 2007-09-06
that would depend on what language you use. In bengali its ram.. in samskrit supposed to be rama, but both are right you know..
user [19] · 2007-09-06
in russia they write XAPE instead or HARE.
i guess they dont say xape xape.
user [39] · 2007-09-06
XAPE written in cyrillic is pronounced the same way as HARE written in latin.
user [78] · 2007-09-07
Wow! cool...
user [38] · 2007-09-08
Actually X in Russian Xape is different from English H but there is no corresponding sound in English. In Czech we transcribe it as Chare.
user [39] · 2007-09-08
[quote][cite] VEDA:[/cite]Actually X in Russian Xape is different from English H but there is no corresponding sound in English. In Czech we transcribe it as Chare.[/quote]

Well, yes, it actually is, if you go in phonetic details. Same as Russian "P" is a different sound than english "R", and Russian "E" is a different sound from English "E". Languages are amazing, arent they? But wait, in Hindi there are actually three ways to pronounce "T" and four ways to pronounce "D"!!! Far out :)
user [39] · 2007-09-08
[quote][cite] Saryu:[/cite]XAPE written in cyrillic is pronounced the same way as HARE written in latin.[/quote]

I was just making a point that Russian "XAPE" is actually pronounced as "Hare" and not "Kzape" ;)
